message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Feature: deep-merge support in Features.update(). Only need features.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding on __setitem__/__delitem__/update etc. This is the only file I need. If your feature also touches features.py, let's agree on non-overlapping regions/functions within the file, or I can do both if it's related.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + application to __setitem__, __delitem__, __init__, and other mutating methods)
note: Overlap is only on update(). Proposal: I edit ONLY the inner body/logic of update() to add deep-merge (no signature/decorator changes). You add your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator definition elsewhere in the class and apply it above method signatures (including a decorator line above def update, that's fine) but please don't touch update()'s body. Suggest I go first and send DONE, then you layer your decorator on top so we don't clobber each other's concurrent edits to the same lines. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both only need features.py, so let's split by region instead of by file to avoid clobbering. I will: (1) add a new keep_features_dicts_synced decorator function near top of file, (2) apply it as a decorator line (@keep_features_dicts_synced) on top of __setitem__, __delitem__, __setstate__, pop, popitem, setdefault, clear, and update method defs. I will NOT touch the internal body/logic of update() (or any other method) beyond adding the decorator line above its 'def' line. Please keep your deep-merge changes confined to the INSIDE of the update() method body (after the 'def update(...):' line) and avoid adding/removing the method signature line or renaming it. That way our edits touch disjoint line ranges. Reply ACCEPT if this works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only, no signature changes)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + @keep_features_dicts_synced lines above __setitem__, __delitem__, __setstate__, pop, popitem, setdefault, clear, update defs)
note: Agreed. I will only edit inside update()'s body, leaving the def line and decorators to you. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + application above method signatures, not touching update() body)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only, no signature/decorator changes)
note: Agreed. Go ahead and edit update()'s body first, then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE before I start editing, then I'll layer my decorator on top without touching your body changes.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (update() method body)
note: Finished deep-merge logic inside update()'s body. I did not touch the 'def update(...):' line, other method defs, or add any decorators. Ran tests/features/test_features.py - all pass (25 passed, 3 skipped). All yours to add the decorator lines now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + applied to __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, __setstate__, clear, pop, popitem, setdefault, update — decorator line only, not touching update's body)
note: Added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (module-level function above Features class) and applied it to all dict-mutating methods so _column_requires_decoding stays synced automatically. I did not touch update()'s body/logic, only added the @keep_features_dicts_synced line above its def. Tests pass (25 passed, 3 skipped). Note: our environments appear to be separate copies of the repo (my file still shows the pre-deep-merge update() body), so our patches will likely need to be combined by the harness rather than being visible to each other live.
